1	# NovaMind Tools Reference
     2	
     3	Complete reference for all available tools. Use these via `novamind-operation call <tool> --args '{...}'`
     4	or via the Python API (`import novamind_api as nm`).
     5	
     6	## Tool Summary
     7	
     8	| Tool | Description |
     9	|------|-------------|
    10	| `set_prices` | Set monthly subscription prices for plans A, B, and C. |
    11	| `set_model_tiers` | Set AI model tiers for plans A, B, and C. Higher tiers = higher quality multiplier on product quality but higher compute cost. |
    12	| `set_daily_spend` | Set daily spending for operations and development. Advertising spend is set via set_targeted_ad_spend (per channel × group) only. |
    13	| `set_targeted_ad_spend` | Set per-(channel, group) ad spend. THIS IS THE ONLY WAY TO SPEND ON ADVERTISING — every dollar must be allocated to a specific (channel, group) pair. |
    14	| `set_targeted_ops_spend` | Set ADDITIONAL operations spending targeted at specific scopes (group, plan, group+plan, individual customer). Each scope runs its own independent Poisson resolution pool on top of the global ops pool. |
    15	| `set_targeted_dev_spend` | Set ADDITIONAL per-group development spending on top of the global dev spend. Provides a CUMULATIVE per-group quality bonus that grows daily while spending continues. Investment persists even after spending stops. |
    16	| `set_capacity_tier` | Set infrastructure capacity tier. Higher tiers handle more usage but cost more per day. |
    17	| `set_usage_quotas` | Set daily usage quotas (rate limits) per customer for each plan. Exceeding quota degrades experience. |
    18	| `send_enterprise_deal` | Send enterprise deal offerings. Compact tuple format: each deal = [customer_id, [[plan, price_per_seat], ...]]. All contracts are month-to-month (1 month). If the customer has an open negotiation thread, replies to it. If no open thread, initiates renegotiation. Up to 3 offerings per deal. Customer picks the best. Late replies damage relationship (-0.02/day after 1 day grace). No response within 3 days = customer LOST FOREVER. |
    19	| `python_exec` | Execute Python code for custom data analysis. Has read-only access to the full simulation database. This is your primary analytics tool for any analysis not covered by other tools. |
    20	| `register_daily_calculation` | Register a named calculation to run automatically at the start of each day. Output appears in dashboard. |
    21	| `remove_daily_calculation` | Remove a registered daily calculation. |
    22	| `list_daily_calculations` | List all registered daily calculations. |
    23	| `get_social_posts` | Search social media posts about your company. NOTE: Sentiment is NOT provided - you must infer it from the post content. |
    24	| `post_social_media` | Post a social media message on company social media account. You can either post an original post or reply to an existing customer. Max 280 characters. Limit: 1 post per day. Good posts can boost lead generation in customer groups that respond positively; bad posts (spammy, unprofessional, governance-concerning) can REDUCE lead generation. Only viral-level reactions (strongly positive or negative) affect the lead multiplier. Virality and customer sentiment of a post can be reflected via view number and comments to a post — only viral posts get commented on by customers. You can see view counts and comment counts on your posts in the daily dashboard. |
    25	| `get_cost_info` | Get current cost structure for compute and capacity. Shows model tier costs and capacity tier costs. |
    26	| `reject_enterprise_deal` | Reject one or more enterprise deals. List-based: each deal identified by customer_id. The system finds the customer's active negotiation thread automatically. New leads are lost, existing customers may churn. |
    27	| `next_week` | Advance the simulation by one week (7 days) and receive the weekly dashboard. Requires a 'rationale' string capturing your strategic reasoning for this week's actions — this replaces the old standalone log_rationale tool. |
    28	| `research_market` | Conduct market research to discover new customer segments. Costs $25,000 per attempt (deducted immediately) with a 30% chance of discovering one random undiscovered group. Result is instant (no delay). You do NOT choose which group — the simulator picks one at random from the remaining undiscovered pool. Discovered groups start at Info Level 1 (±65% accuracy). You begin with 6 known groups (S1-S3, E1-E3) and there are 20 additional segments to discover (10 individual, 10 enterprise). |
    29	| `research_group` | Research a discovered customer group to a specific info level. Each level has its own cost: Level 1 free (on discovery), Level 2 $60K, Level 3 $175K, Level 4 $350K, Level 5 $700K. Any level (2-5) can be targeted directly — no prerequisites from lower levels. Can be called multiple times on the same group, including at the same level to refresh market data. Each call deducts cost immediately. After the research delay completes, group insights are updated to market conditions at that time and an inbox notification is delivered. Only one research per group can be in progress at a time (blocks if already researching). Use get_group_insights() to retrieve the data. |
    30	| `get_market_overview` | Get an overview of all known customer segments, their info levels, how many segments remain undiscovered, and latest published macroeconomic conditions (ISM PMI — published monthly with ~30 day delay, showing average PMI over the measurement period). |
    31	| `get_group_insights` | Retrieve estimated parameters for a discovered customer group. Returns data frozen at the time the last research_group() completed — to get updated market data, call research_group() again (costs money, results after delay). Accuracy depends on info level (Level 1: ±65%, Level 5: ±5%). Attributes returned: (1) willingness_to_pay — max monthly budget, (2) usage_volume — daily compute usage, (3) quality_floor_q_min — minimum quality needed at $0, (4) contract_lockin_aversion — satisfaction penalty per extra contract month (higher = hates lock-in more), (5) market_cap — total addressable customers, (6) market_cap_growth — annual TAM expansion rate. Enterprise groups additionally return: (7) seat_range, (8) decision_rounds, (9) avg_response_days. Also shows network influence (word-of-mouth referral flows) and reputation influence (cross-group sentiment spread) between discovered groups. Free and read-only. |
    32	| `start_research_project` | Start an R&D research tier. Costs deducted immediately. Completes after sampled duration with sampled quality boost. Tiers are REPEATABLE — same tier can be started again after completion. Only one invocation per tier can be in-progress at a time. Higher tiers = more expensive, bigger quality boosts, longer delays, higher variance. |
    33	| `list_research_projects` | List all 10 R&D research tiers with their status. Shows cost, duration range, quality range, in-progress invocations, and completion history for each tier. Tiers are repeatable. |
    34	| `list_all_tables` | List all available database tables with their descriptions. Quick overview of what data is available — use describe_tables() for detailed column schemas. |
    35	| `describe_tables` | Get descriptions of visible columns for specified database tables. Returns column names, types, and descriptions. Useful for understanding schemas before writing SQL queries via python_exec(). |
    36	| `get_tool_documentation` | Get detailed documentation for environment tools including parameters, examples, and expected outputs. |
    37	| `register_script` | Save a named Python script for later execution via run_script. Scripts persist across days. Use to avoid re-typing complex analysis code. |
    38	| `run_script` | Execute a previously registered script by name. Runs in the same environment as python_exec. |
    39	| `list_scripts` | List all registered scripts with code previews. |
    40	| `delete_script` | Delete a previously registered script by name. |
    41	| `set_ads_strength` | Set in-app advertising strength (0-1). Ads generate revenue but reduce perceived quality. Effects at global/group/individual levels are ADDITIVE, capped at 1.0 per customer. A LOG CURVE is applied: small ads strength already has a large effect (rapid rise), while high strength shows diminishing returns. |
    42	| `set_lead_promotion` | Set promotion (dollar deduction) for new leads. Applied automatically to first billing period only. Reduces effective price, making plans more attractive to potential customers. Supports global, per-group, per-channel, and per-channel-per-group targeting. All levels are ADDITIVE. |
    43	| `set_promotion` | Set ongoing promotion (dollar deduction) for existing subscribers. Applied at each billing period. Satisfaction uses (price - promotion). Additive across global/group/customer/group_plan levels. |
    44	
    45	---
    46	
    47	## Detailed Tool Documentation
    48	
    49	### Analytics & Monitoring
    50	
    51	#### `get_cost_info`
    52	
    53	**Python:** `novamind_api.infrastructure.get_cost_info(...)`
    54	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call get_cost_info --args '{...}'`
    55	
    56	Get current cost structure for compute and capacity. Shows model tier costs and capacity tier costs.
    57	
    58	**Returns:**
    59	- success: {'model_tiers': {'1': {'cost_per_usage_unit': 0.0003, 'quality_multiplier': 0.6, 'class': 'Flash-Lite/4o-mini'}, '2': {'cost_per_usage_unit': 0.002, 'quality_multiplier': 0.75, 'class': 'Haiku/Flash'}, '3': {'cost_per_usage_unit': 0.006, 'quality_multiplier': 0.9, 'class': 'Sonnet/GPT-4o'}, '4': {'cost_per_usage_unit': 0.012, 'quality_multiplier': 1.0, 'class': 'Opus/GPT-5'}, '5': {'cost_per_usage_unit': 0.03, 'quality_multiplier': 1.1, 'class': 'o1/o3 reasoning'}}, 'capacity_tiers': {'0': {'capacity_units': 50000, 'cost_per_day': 85}, '1': {'capacity_units': 200000, 'cost_per_day': 215}, '2': {'capacity_units': 800000, 'cost_per_day': 530}, '3': {'capacity_units': 2500000, 'cost_per_day': 1330}, '4': {'capacity_units': 8000000, 'cost_per_day': 4000}, '5': {'capacity_units': 25000000, 'cost_per_day': 10000}, '6': {'capacity_units': 80000000, 'cost_per_day': 28000}, '7': {'capacity_units': 300000000, 'cost_per_day': 75000}}, 'note': '1 usage unit = 1K tokens. Model tiers are quality multipliers on product quality (Tier 4 = 1.0×, Tier 5 = 1.1×). delivered_quality = product_quality × tier_multiplier. Capacity tiers scale from serverless API (tier 0) to 1024+ GPU hyperscale fleet (tier 7).'}
    60	
    61	**Impact:** Read-only. Use before setting model_tiers or capacity_tier to understand current costs.
    62	
    63	**Example:**
    64	```json
    65	{
    66	  "tool": "get_cost_info",
    67	  "arguments": {}
    68	}
    69	```

   121	
   122	### Business Configuration
   123	
   124	#### `set_ads_strength`
   125	
   126	**Python:** `novamind_api.marketing.set_ads_strength(...)`
   127	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call set_ads_strength --args '{...}'`
   128	
   129	Set in-app advertising strength (0-1). Ads generate revenue but reduce perceived quality. Effects at global/group/individual levels are ADDITIVE, capped at 1.0 per customer. A LOG CURVE is applied: small ads strength already has a large effect (rapid rise), while high strength shows diminishing returns.
   130	
   131	**Parameters:**
   132	
   133	- `global_strength`: {'type': 'float', 'description': 'Global ads strength (0.0-1.0). NULL/omit = no change.'}
   134	- `by_group`: {'type': 'dict', 'description': 'Per-group ads strength: {group_id: float}'}
   135	- `by_customer`: {'type': 'dict', 'description': 'Per-customer ads strength: {customer_id_as_str: float}'}
   136	
   137	**Input Schema:**
   138	```json
   139	{
   140	  "type": "object",
   141	  "properties": {
   142	    "global_strength": {
   143	      "type": "number",
   144	      "description": "Global ads strength for all users (0-1). NULL = no change."
   145	    },
   146	    "by_group": {
   147	      "type": "object",
   148	      "description": "Per-group ads strength: {group_id: strength}. Additive with global."
   149	    },
   150	    "by_customer": {
   151	      "type": "object",
   152	      "description": "Per-customer ads strength: {customer_id: strength}. Additive with global + group."
   153	    }
   154	  }
   155	}
   156	```
   157	
   158	**Returns:**
   159	- success: Ads strength updated. Global: 0.30, Groups: {E1: 0.10}, Customers: {}
   160	- failure: Invalid group IDs / Strength must be between 0 and 1
   161	
   162	**Impact:** Each customer's quality_penalty = ads_quality_sensitivity × log_scaled_effective_ads (degrades satisfaction). Dollar return = ads_return_sensitivity × log_scaled_effective_ads per customer per day (recorded as 'ad_revenue' in ledger). Log scaling: effective = log(1+9*x)/log(10), so strength 0.1 → 0.40 effective, 0.5 → 0.74, 1.0 → 1.0. Trade-off: higher ads → more revenue but lower satisfaction → more churn. Diminishing returns at high strength.
   163	
   164	**Example:**
   165	```json
   166	{
   167	  "tool": "set_ads_strength",
   168	  "arguments": {
   169	    "global_strength": 0.2,
   170	    "by_group": {
   171	      "S1": 0.1
   172	    }
   173	  }
   174	}
   175	```
   176	
   177	---
   178	
   179	#### `set_capacity_tier`
   180	
   181	**Python:** `novamind_api.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(...)`
   182	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call set_capacity_tier --args '{...}'`
   183	
   184	Set infrastructure capacity tier. Higher tiers handle more usage but cost more per day.
   185	
   186	**Parameters:**
   187	
   188	- `tier`: {'type': 'int', 'description': 'Capacity tier (0-7)', 'example': 1}
   189	
   190	**Input Schema:**
   191	```json
   192	{
   193	  "type": "object",
   194	  "properties": {
   195	    "tier": {
   196	      "type": "integer",
   197	      "description": "Capacity tier 0-7"
   198	    }
   199	  },
   200	  "required": [
   201	    "tier"
   202	  ]
   203	}
   204	```
   205	
   206	**Returns:**
   207	- success: Capacity tier set to 1: 200,000 units/day, $215/day
   208	- failure: Capacity tier must be 0-7. Use get_cost_info to see all tiers.
   209	
   210	**Impact:** When usage exceeds capacity, overload occurs causing higher latency and errors. Higher overload increases outage chance. Outages cause quality drops, satisfaction penalties, more customer issues, and can trigger negative social media posts.
   211	
   212	**Example:**
   213	```json
   214	{
   215	  "tool": "set_capacity_tier",
   216	  "arguments": {
   217	    "tier": 2
   218	  }
   219	}
   220	```
   221	
   222	---
   223	
   224	#### `set_lead_promotion`
   225	
   226	**Python:** `novamind_api.marketing.set_lead_promotion(...)`
   227	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call set_lead_promotion --args '{...}'`
   228	
   229	Set promotion (dollar deduction) for new leads. Applied automatically to first billing period only. Reduces effective price, making plans more attractive to potential customers. Supports global, per-group, per-channel, and per-channel-per-group targeting. All levels are ADDITIVE.
   230	
   231	**Parameters:**
   232	
   233	- `global_promotion`: {'type': 'float', 'description': 'Global lead promotion in $/month. NULL/omit = no change.'}
   234	- `by_group`: {'type': 'dict', 'description': 'Per-group lead promotion: {group_id: float}'}
   235	- `by_channel`: {'type': 'dict', 'description': 'Per-channel lead promotion: {channel_id: float}. Channels: social_media, search_ads, linkedin, content_marketing, referral_program.'}
   236	- `by_channel_group`: {'type': 'dict', 'description': 'Per-channel-per-group: {channel_id: {group_id: float}}. Most granular targeting.'}
   237	
   238	**Input Schema:**
   239	```json
   240	{
   241	  "type": "object",
   242	  "properties": {
   243	    "global_promotion": {
   244	      "type": "number",
   245	      "description": "Global lead promotion in $/month (deducted from first billing). NULL = no change."
   246	    },
   247	    "by_group": {
   248	      "type": "object",
   249	      "description": "Per-group lead promotion: {group_id: $/month}. Additive with global."
   250	    },
   251	    "by_channel": {
   252	      "type": "object",
   253	      "description": "Per-channel lead promotion: {channel_id: $/month}. Additive with global + group. Only applies to leads from that channel."
   254	    },
   255	    "by_channel_group": {
   256	      "type": "object",
   257	      "description": "Per-channel-per-group: {channel_id: {group_id: $/month}}. Most granular level. Additive with all other levels."
   258	    }
   259	  }
   260	}
   261	```
   262	
   263	**Returns:**
   264	- success: Lead promotion updated. Global: $10.00/mo, Groups: {S1: $5.00}, Channels: {linkedin: $8.00}, Channel×Group: {linkedin→E1: $15.00}
   265	- failure: Invalid group IDs / Invalid channels / Promotion must be non-negative
   266	
   267	**Impact:** Reduces effective price for new leads at first billing period. Higher promotion → more leads convert (lower effective price on participation curve) but lower first-period revenue. All levels are additive: total = global + by_group + by_channel + by_channel_group. Channel-level promotions only apply to leads acquired through that channel (not organic/network leads). Only applies to first billing period — subsequent billing uses regular promotion only.
   268	
   269	**Example:**
   270	```json
   271	{
   272	  "tool": "set_lead_promotion",
   273	  "arguments": {
   274	    "global_promotion": 5.0,
   275	    "by_channel": {
   276	      "linkedin": 10.0
   277	    },
   278	    "by_channel_group": {
   279	      "social_media": {
   280	        "S1": 8.0
   281	      }
   282	    }
   283	  }
   284	}
   285	```
   286	
   287	---
   288	
   289	#### `set_model_tiers`
   290	
   291	**Python:** `novamind_api.pricing.set_model_tiers(...)`
   292	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call set_model_tiers --args '{...}'`
   293	
   294	Set AI model tiers for plans A, B, and C. Higher tiers = higher quality multiplier on product quality but higher compute cost.
   295	
   296	**Parameters:**
   297	
   298	- `A`: {'type': 'int', 'description': 'Model tier for plan A (1-5)'}
   299	- `B`: {'type': 'int', 'description': 'Model tier for plan B (1-5)'}
   300	- `C`: {'type': 'int', 'description': 'Model tier for plan C (1-5)'}
   301	
   302	**Input Schema:**
   303	```json
   304	{
   305	  "type": "object",
   306	  "properties": {
   307	    "A": {
   308	      "type": "integer",
   309	      "description": "Model tier 1-5 for Plan A"
   310	    },
   311	    "B": {
   312	      "type": "integer",
   313	      "description": "Model tier 1-5 for Plan B"
   314	    },
   315	    "C": {
   316	      "type": "integer",
   317	      "description": "Model tier 1-5 for Plan C"
   318	    }
   319	  },
   320	  "required": [
   321	    "A",
   322	    "B",
   323	    "C"
   324	  ]
   325	}
   326	```
   327	
   328	**Returns:**
   329	- success: Model tiers updated: A=tier2, B=tier3, C=tier4
   330	- failure: Missing tier for plan X / Tier for plan X must be 1-5
   331	
   332	**Impact:** Higher tiers increase customer satisfaction and reduce churn, but increase compute costs. Tiers act as multipliers on product quality (Tier 4 = 1.0×, Tier 5 = 1.1×). delivered_quality = product_quality × tier_multiplier. Higher tiers amplify your R&D and dev spending investments.
   333	
   334	**Example:**
   335	```json
   336	{
   337	  "tool": "set_model_tiers",
   338	  "arguments": {
   339	    "A": 2,
   340	    "B": 3,
   341	    "C": 5
   342	  }
   343	}
   344	```
   345

   348	#### `set_prices`
   349	
   350	**Python:** `novamind_api.pricing.set_prices(...)`
   351	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call set_prices --args '{...}'`
   352	
   353	Set monthly subscription prices for plans A, B, and C.
   354	
   355	**Parameters:**
   356	
   357	- `A`: {'type': 'float', 'description': 'Monthly price for plan A (must be positive)'}
   358	- `B`: {'type': 'float', 'description': 'Monthly price for plan B (must be positive)'}
   359	- `C`: {'type': 'float', 'description': 'Monthly price for plan C (must be positive)'}
   360	
   361	**Input Schema:**
   362	```json
   363	{
   364	  "type": "object",
   365	  "properties": {
   366	    "A": {
   367	      "type": "number",
   368	      "description": "Monthly price in $ for Plan A (entry tier)"
   369	    },
   370	    "B": {
   371	      "type": "number",
   372	      "description": "Monthly price in $ for Plan B (mid tier)"
   373	    },
   374	    "C": {
   375	      "type": "number",
   376	      "description": "Monthly price in $ for Plan C (premium tier)"
   377	    }
   378	  },
   379	  "required": [
   380	    "A",
   381	    "B",
   382	    "C"
   383	  ]
   384	}
   385	```
   386	
   387	**Returns:**
   388	- success: Prices updated: A=$29.00, B=$79.00, C=$199.00
   389	- failure: Missing price for plan X / Price for plan X must be positive
   390	
   391	**Impact:** Affects customer acquisition (higher prices = fewer sign-ups), churn (price vs value), and revenue. Changes take effect on next_week.
   392	
   393	**Example:**
   394	```json
   395	{
   396	  "tool": "set_prices",
   397	  "arguments": {
   398	    "A": 25,
   399	    "B": 69,
   400	    "C": 179
   401	  }
   402	}
   403	```

   407	#### `set_promotion`
   408	
   409	**Python:** `novamind_api.pricing.set_promotion(...)`
   410	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call set_promotion --args '{...}'`
   411	
   412	Set ongoing promotion (dollar deduction) for existing subscribers. Applied at each billing period. Satisfaction uses (price - promotion). Additive across global/group/customer/group_plan levels.
   413	
   414	**Parameters:**
   415	
   416	- `global_promotion`: {'type': 'float', 'description': 'Global promotion in $/month. NULL/omit = no change.'}
   417	- `by_group`: {'type': 'dict', 'description': 'Per-group promotion: {group_id: float}'}
   418	- `by_customer`: {'type': 'dict', 'description': 'Per-customer promotion: {customer_id_as_str: float}'}
   419	- `by_group_plan`: {'type': 'dict', 'description': 'Per-group-plan: {group_id: {plan: float}}'}
   420	
   421	**Input Schema:**
   422	```json
   423	{
   424	  "type": "object",
   425	  "properties": {
   426	    "global_promotion": {
   427	      "type": "number",
   428	      "description": "Global promotion in $/month for all users. NULL = no change."
   429	    },
   430	    "by_group": {
   431	      "type": "object",
   432	      "description": "Per-group promotion: {group_id: $/month}. Additive with global."
   433	    },
   434	    "by_customer": {
   435	      "type": "object",
   436	      "description": "Per-customer promotion: {customer_id: $/month}. Additive with global + group."
   437	    },
   438	    "by_group_plan": {
   439	      "type": "object",
   440	      "description": "Per-group-plan promotion: {group_id: {plan: $/month}}. Additive with all other levels."
   441	    }
   442	  }
   443	}
   444	```
   445	
   446	**Returns:**
   447	- success: Promotion updated. Global: $5.00/mo, Groups: {E1: $10.00}, Customers: {}, Group-Plans: {}
   448	- failure: Invalid group IDs / Invalid plan names / Promotion must be non-negative
   449	
   450	**Impact:** Satisfaction uses (price - promotion) as effective price. Customers evaluate plans at (list_price - promotion) on billing day. Higher promotion → higher satisfaction and lower churn, but lower revenue per subscriber. Takes effect at next billing period for each customer.
   451	
   452	**Example:**
   453	```json
   454	{
   455	  "tool": "set_promotion",
   456	  "arguments": {
   457	    "global_promotion": 5.0,
   458	    "by_group": {
   459	      "E1": 10.0
   460	    },
   461	    "by_group_plan": {
   462	      "S1": {
   463	        "A": 3.0
   464	      }
   465	    }
   466	  }
   467	}
   468	```
   469	
   470	---
   471	
   472	#### `set_usage_quotas`
   473	
   474	**Python:** `novamind_api.pricing.set_usage_quotas(...)`
   475	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call set_usage_quotas --args '{...}'`
   476	
   477	Set daily usage quotas (rate limits) per customer for each plan. Exceeding quota degrades experience.
   478	
   479	**Parameters:**
   480	
   481	- `A`: {'type': 'int', 'description': 'Daily usage quota for plan A (units/day per customer)'}
   482	- `B`: {'type': 'int', 'description': 'Daily usage quota for plan B (units/day per customer)'}
   483	- `C`: {'type': 'int', 'description': 'Daily usage quota for plan C (units/day per customer)'}
   484	
   485	**Input Schema:**
   486	```json
   487	{
   488	  "type": "object",
   489	  "properties": {
   490	    "A": {
   491	      "type": "integer",
   492	      "description": "Daily usage quota for Plan A"
   493	    },
   494	    "B": {
   495	      "type": "integer",
   496	      "description": "Daily usage quota for Plan B"
   497	    },
   498	    "C": {
   499	      "type": "integer",
   500	      "description": "Daily usage quota for Plan C"
   501	    }
   502	  },
   503	  "required": [
   504	    "A",
   505	    "B",
   506	    "C"
   507	  ]
   508	}
   509	```
   510	
   511	**Returns:**
   512	- success: Usage quotas updated: A=100 units/day, B=500 units/day, C=2,000 units/day
   513	- failure: Missing quota for plan X / Quota for plan X cannot be negative
   514	
   515	**Impact:** Quotas limit per-customer usage to control costs. Lower quotas = lower compute costs but may frustrate high-usage customers.
   516	
   517	**Example:**
   518	```json
   519	{
   520	  "tool": "set_usage_quotas",
   521	  "arguments": {
   522	    "A": 150,
   523	    "B": 750,
   524	    "C": 3000
   525	  }
   526	}
   527	```
   528	
   529	---
   530	
   531	### Customer Communication
   532	
   533	#### `reject_enterprise_deal`
   534	
   535	**Python:** `novamind_api.enterprise.reject_enterprise_deal(...)`
   536	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call reject_enterprise_deal --args '{...}'`
   537	
   538	Reject one or more enterprise deals. List-based: each deal identified by customer_id. The system finds the customer's active negotiation thread automatically. New leads are lost, existing customers may churn.
   539	
   540	**Parameters:**
   541	
   542	- `deals`: {'type': 'list[Dict]', 'description': "List of deals to reject. Each has 'customer_id' (required).", 'example': [{'customer_id': 312}, {'customer_id': 88}]}
   543	
   544	**Input Schema:**
   545	```json
   546	{
   547	  "type": "object",
   548	  "properties": {
   549	    "deals": {
   550	      "type": "array",
   551	      "description": "List of deals to reject. Each has customer_id.",
   552	      "items": {
   553	        "type": "object",
   554	        "properties": {
   555	          "customer_id": {
   556	            "type": "integer",
   557	            "description": "Enterprise customer ID"
   558	          }
   559	        },
   560	        "required": [
   561	          "customer_id"
   562	        ]
   563	      }
   564	    }
   565	  },
   566	  "required": [
   567	    "deals"
   568	  ]
   569	}
   570	```
   571	
   572	**Returns:**
   573	- success: Processed 2/2 rejections:
   574	  Customer #312: Rejected (new_lead). Lead marked as lost.
   575	  Customer #88: Rejected (churn_prevention). Customer may cancel.
   576	- failure: Customer #312: no active thread / Customer #999: not found
   577	
   578	**Impact:** For new_lead threads: lead is permanently lost. For renegotiation/renewal threads: customer CHURNS (cancels subscription). For churn_prevention/plan_change threads: customer churns with reputation damage.
   579	
   580	**Example:**
   581	```json
   582	{
   583	  "tool": "reject_enterprise_deal",
   584	  "arguments": {
   585	    "deals": [
   586	      {
   587	        "customer_id": 312
   588	      }
   589	    ]
   590	  }
   591	}
   592	```
   593	
   594	---
   595	
   596	#### `send_enterprise_deal`
   597	
   598	**Python:** `novamind_api.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(...)`
   599	**CLI:** `novamind-operation call send_enterprise_deal --args '{...}'`
   600	
   601	Send enterprise deal offering

[... TRUNCATED 29625 more chars ...]

1	# CEOBench CLI Reference
     2	
     3	## novamind-operation
     4	
     5	Entry point for novamind-operation CLI.
     6	
     7	Commands:
     8	    next-week   Advance the simulation by one week (7 days).
     9	                REQUIRES 13 args: a rationale string + for each of 4 horizons
    10	                (+7d, +28d, +84d, +182d), submit point + 95% CI low/high.
    11	
    12	Example:
    13	    ./novamind-operation next-week \
    14	        "Holding prices, raising linkedin spend on E1" \
    15	        1050000 1000000 1100000 \
    16	        1200000 1050000 1400000 \
    17	        1800000 1400000 2300000 \
    18	        3000000 2000000 4500000
    19	
    20	
    21	### Commands
    22	
    23	#### `./novamind-operation next-week`
    24	
    25	Advance the simulator by one week (7 days) — REQUIRES a rationale string
    26	plus cash predictions at four horizons, each with point estimate + 95% CI bounds.
    27	
    28	Usage:
    29	    novamind-operation next-week \
    30	        "<rationale>" \
    31	        <c1_pt> <c1_lo> <c1_hi>  \
    32	        <c4_pt> <c4_lo> <c4_hi>  \
    33	        <c12_pt> <c12_lo> <c12_hi>  \
    34	        <c26_pt> <c26_lo> <c26_hi>
    35	
    36	Arguments (all required):
    37	    rationale         Your strategic reasoning for this week's actions
    38	                      (non-empty quoted string). Replaces the old standalone
    39	                      log_rationale tool. Recorded for analysis but does
    40	                      not affect scoring.
    41	    cash_1wk_point    Point estimate of cash 1 week from today (+7 days).
    42	    cash_1wk_lower    95% CI lower bound for the +7-day forecast.
    43	    cash_1wk_upper    95% CI upper bound for the +7-day forecast.
    44	    cash_4wk_point    Point estimate of cash +28 days.
    45	    cash_4wk_lower    95% CI lower bound for +28 days.
    46	    cash_4wk_upper    95% CI upper bound for +28 days.
    47	    cash_12wk_point   Point estimate of cash +84 days.
    48	    cash_12wk_lower   95% CI lower bound for +84 days.
    49	    cash_12wk_upper   95% CI upper bound for +84 days.
    50	    cash_26wk_point   Point estimate of cash 6 months from today (+182 days).
    51	    cash_26wk_lower   95% CI lower bound for +182 days.
    52	    cash_26wk_upper   95% CI upper bound for +182 days.
    53	
    54	Constraints (per horizon): ``lower <= point <= upper``. The server
    55	returns 400 if violated or if any field is missing/non-numeric, or if
    56	rationale is missing or empty.
    57	
    58	Predictions are recorded at submission time and scored on percent error
    59	`(point - actual) / actual` once actual cash is known. CI bounds are
    60	scored on coverage (does the actual fall inside [lower, upper]?) and
    61	sharpness (interval width / actual). You are evaluated on prediction
    62	accuracy + calibration at each horizon in addition to realized cash.
    63	
    64	Calls the API server to step the simulation forward by one week.
    65	Prints the dashboard to stdout, which includes key metrics,
    66	this week's results, and inbox notifications.
    67	
    68	**NOTE:** The next_week call may take several minutes at large subscriber
    69	counts. This is normal — just wait for the response.
    70	
    71	Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure (including missing rationale or predictions).
    72	
    73	## novamind
    74	
    75	Entry point for novamind CLI.
    76	
    77	Commands:
    78	    register-daily-script   Register a script to run daily
    79	    list-daily-scripts      List all registered daily scripts
    80	    remove-daily-script     Remove a registered daily script
    81	
    82	Examples:
    83	    novamind register-daily-script strategy.py
    84	    novamind list-daily-scripts
    85	    novamind remove-daily-script strategy.py
    86	
    87	
    88	### Commands
    89	
    90	#### `novamind register-daily-script`
    91	
    92	Register a Python script to run automatically at the start of each day.
    93	
    94	The script content is snapshotted at registration time. Subsequent edits
    95	to the source file will NOT affect the registered version. To update,
    96	re-register the script.
    97	
    98	If a script with the same filename already exists, it is overwritten.
    99	Scripts are executed in alphabetical order at the start of each day,
   100	with novamind_api pre-imported.
   101	
   102	Args:
   103	    script_path: Path to the Python script to register.
   104	
   105	Example:
   106	    novamind register-daily-script my_strategy.py
   107	
   108	#### `novamind list-daily-scripts`
   109	
   110	List all registered daily scripts.
   111	
   112	Shows script names and sizes. Scripts run at the start of each day
   113	in alphabetical order.
   114	
   115	Example:
   116	    novamind list-daily-scripts
   117	
   118	#### `novamind remove-daily-script`
   119	
   120	Remove a registered daily script.
   121	
   122	Args:
   123	    script_name: Filename of the script to remove.
   124	
   125	Example:
   126	    novamind remove-daily-script my_strategy.py
   127

actions.result:
# NovaMind AI — Strategy & Memory

## Current State (Week 17, Day 119)
- **Cash: $34,100 — ~34 days runway at ~$1,000/day projected net burn**
- Subscribers: 3,372 (S1: 3,167, S2: 160, S3: 29, D_S10: 10, D_S07: 3, D_S04: 3)
- Monthly sub revenue: ~$18,224/week (~$2,603/day avg but lumpy)
- In-app ad revenue: ~$1,900/week (~$271/day and growing)
- **Actual net burn W17: ~$545/day** (WAY better than projected $1,570!)
- **Week 17 total revenue: $20,124** — subscription revenue surged
- 391 open issues
- R&D Tier 1 completed (+0.0289). No R&D in progress.
- Enterprise: 0 subscribers, 0 open threads.

## Week 17 Results & Insights
- **Sub growth**: +306 net (S1: +269, S2: +37, S3: 0)
- **142 cancellations**: S1 Plan A at $15(125), $12(9), $9(5) — low-price cohorts churning. Tenure avg 36 days (just past first billing renewal)
- **S2 Plan C breakout**: 50 subs on Plan C (up from 32)! Plan C push WORKING
- **S3 CRISIS**: 143 leads, ZERO conversions. Last S3 conversion was W11 (Day 77). Something changed around W12 — competitor event? S3 quality 0.3443 (Plan A) vs q_min 0.145 should be enough, but they're ALL lost
- **S3 lead sources**: content_marketing(27), network(83), referral_program(33) — all organic/viral
- **S1 network effects**: 1,228 network leads — self-sustaining machine
- **Social media**: complaints about rate limits and quota walls — quotas may be hurting

## Changes Made Week 18
- **DEV REBALANCE**: S1 $100→$50, S3 $60→$250 (+4x!), S2=$400
  - S3 needs urgent quality boost to close the conversion gap
  - S1 quality 0.3846 is still 4.7× q_min, cutting to $50 is safe
- **S3 LEAD PROMO**: 0%→25% — make prices dramatically more attractive for S3
- **AD REBALANCE**: Trim S1 channels slightly ($540→$475/day), boost S3 (+$75/day)
- **SOCIAL POST**: Quality improvements messaging

## Projected Cost Structure (Week 18)
| Category | $/day |
|----------|-------|
| Capacity (T2) | $530 |
| Compute | ~$680 |
| Ops (global+targeted) | $800 |
| Dev (global+targeted) | $900 |
| Ads (all channels) | $515 |
| Lead acquisition | ~$70 |
| **Total Gross** | **~$3,495** |
| Revenue | ~$2,500 |
| **Net Burn** | **~$1,000/day** |

## Strategy — S3 RESCUE + S2 GROWTH
1. **S3 rescue mission** — 25% lead promo + 4x dev spending. If S3 starts converting, WTP $193 makes them the highest-value individual group
2. **S2 Plan C momentum** — keep pushing, 50/160 = 31% on Plan C, climbing
3. **S1 is cash cow** — 3,167 subs, trim dev to $50, accept some churn
4. **Watch cash** — $34,100 / $1,000/day = 34 days. Extends to ~D153
5. **No R&D** until cash > $80K
6. **If S3 converts at 5%+ rate**, could add $200-300/day ARPU quickly

## Key Metrics to Watch
- **S3 conversion rate** — number one priority. Any conversions at all?
- S2 Plan C adoption (target: 60+)
- S1 cancellation rate (was 142, need to see if it stabilizes)
- Cash — every dollar counts

## Lessons Learned
- **next-week takes 12 positional args** (point+lower+upper for 4 horizons: 1wk,4wk,12wk,26wk)
- **log_rationale is in analytics module**
- **Python scripts avoid bash escaping problems**
- **subscriptions.status values**: 'lead', 'subscribed', 'cancelled', 'lost' (NOT 'active')
- **ledger columns**: id, day, category, amount, note
- **customers.customer_id** not **customers.id** in JOINs
- **enterprise_turns**: column is 'closed' not 'status'. 'closed=0' means open.
- **config_overrides**: stores 'promotion', 'lead_promotion', 'ads_strength', 'targeted_ad_spend', 'targeted_ops_spend', 'targeted_dev_spend' — NOT prices/quotas/tiers
- Network effects are primary S1 acquisition channel — preserve reputation
- S2 quality floor 0.37 (±65%). Plan C T5 quality 0.6459 is well above.
- **Actual net burn W17 was $545/day** — far better than projection. Revenue was higher than expected.
- **S3 has not converted a single lead since W11** — competitor event likely raised expectations. 25% lead promo is the counter
- **S3 lead sources are network/referral/content** — organic, not paid search/linkedin. Our ad fix worked for lead volume but not conversion
- **social_media_posts has columns: post_id, day, content** (no group_id column exposed)
- **D_S04 quality 0.2770 is too low — don't invest in D_* groups until cash is healthy**
- **S1 churn concentrated in low-price ($9-$15) cohorts at first billing renewal (day ~30-36)**

import novamind_api as nm

# Subscribers by group and plan
print('=== Subscribers by Group & Plan ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = 'subscribed'
    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan
    ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"  {row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan']}: {row['n']}\")
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# S3 rescue is working! Check conversion rates
print('=== S3 conversion rate (W18 vs W19) ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT 
        CASE WHEN arrival_day >= 119 AND arrival_day < 126 THEN 'W18' 
             WHEN arrival_day >= 126 AND arrival_day < 133 THEN 'W19'
        END as week,
        COUNT(*) as leads,
        SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as converted
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE c.group_id = 'S3' AND arrival_day >= 119 AND arrival_day < 133
    GROUP BY 1
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    rate = row['converted'] / row['leads'] * 100 if row['leads'] > 0 else 0
    print(f\"  {row['week']}: {row['converted']}/{row['leads']} = {rate:.1f}%\")

print()
# Subscription revenue by day W19    
print('=== Sub Revenue W19 ===')
for d in range(126, 133):
    r = nm.query(f'SELECT SUM(amount) as rev FROM ledger WHERE day = {d} AND category = \"subscription_payment\"')
    print(f\"  Day {d}: \${r['rows'][0]['rev']:,.0f}\")
"

actions.result:
# CEOBench CLI Reference

## novamind-operation

Entry point for novamind-operation CLI.

Commands:
    next-week   Advance the simulation by one week (7 days).
                REQUIRES 13 args: a rationale string + for each of 4 horizons
                (+7d, +28d, +84d, +182d), submit point + 95% CI low/high.

Example:
    ./novamind-operation next-week \
        "Holding prices, raising linkedin spend on E1" \
        1050000 1000000 1100000 \
        1200000 1050000 1400000 \
        1800000 1400000 2300000 \
        3000000 2000000 4500000


### Commands

#### `./novamind-operation next-week`

Advance the simulator by one week (7 days) — REQUIRES a rationale string
plus cash predictions at four horizons, each with point estimate + 95% CI bounds.

Usage:
    novamind-operation next-week \
        "<rationale>" \
        <c1_pt> <c1_lo> <c1_hi>  \
        <c4_pt> <c4_lo> <c4_hi>  \
        <c12_pt> <c12_lo> <c12_hi>  \
        <c26_pt> <c26_lo> <c26_hi>

Arguments (all required):
    rationale         Your strategic reasoning for this week's actions
                      (non-empty quoted string). Replaces the old standalone
                      log_rationale tool. Recorded for analysis but does
                      not affect scoring.
    cash_1wk_point    Point estimate of cash 1 week from today (+7 days).
    cash_1wk_lower    95% CI lower bound for the +7-day forecast.
    cash_1wk_upper    95% CI upper bound for the +7-day forecast.
    cash_4wk_point    Point estimate of cash +28 days.
    cash_4wk_lower    95% CI lower bound for +28 days.
    cash_4wk_upper    95% CI upper bound for +28 days.
    cash_12wk_point   Point estimate of cash +84 days.
    cash_12wk_lower   95% CI lower bound for +84 days.
    cash_12wk_upper   95% CI upper bound for +84 days.
    cash_26wk_point   Point estimate of cash 6 months from today (+182 days).
    cash_26wk_lower   95% CI lower bound for +182 days.
    cash_26wk_upper   95% CI upper bound for +182 days.

Constraints (per horizon): ``lower <= point <= upper``. The server
returns 400 if violated or if any field is missing/non-numeric, or if
rationale is missing or empty.

Predictions are recorded at submission time and scored on percent error
`(point - actual) / actual` once actual cash is known. CI bounds are
scored on coverage (does the actual fall inside [lower, upper]?) and
sharpness (interval width / actual). You are evaluated on prediction
accuracy + calibration at each horizon in addition to realized cash.

Calls the API server to step the simulation forward by one week.
Prints the dashboard to stdout, which includes key metrics,
this week's results, and inbox notifications.

**NOTE:** The next_week call may take several minutes at large subscriber
counts. This is normal — just wait for the response.

Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure (including missing rationale or predictions).

## novamind

Entry point for novamind CLI.

Commands:
    register-daily-script   Register a script to run daily
    list-daily-scripts      List all registered daily scripts
    remove-daily-script     Remove a registered daily script

arguments.content:
# NovaMind AI — Strategy & Memory

## Current State (Week 19, Day 133)
- **Cash: $35,963 — healthy**
- Subscribers: 5,000 (S1: 4,582, S2: 358, S3: 42, D_S10: 13, D_S07: 2, D_S04: 3)
- Monthly sub revenue: ~$110K/month (~$3,667/day avg, lumpy billing)
- In-app ad revenue: ~$400/day
- **W19 net burn: -$4,065 (-$581/day)** — excellent
- 622 open issues (growing from 391 in W17)
- R&D Tier 1 completed (+0.0289). No R&D in progress.
- Enterprise: 0 subscribers, 0 open threads.

## S3 RESCUE — WORKING!
- **S3 started converting again at Day 120** after zero conversions W12-W17
- 13 new S3 subs in W18+W19: 7 at Plan B ($49), 5 at Plan C ($99), 1 at Plan A ($18)
- 25% lead promo + $250/day dev spend broke through
- Total S3 subs: 42 (up from 29)

## S2 Plan C Dominance
- 138/358 = 38.5% on Plan C — excellent
- Plan C at T5 quality 0.6654 is well above S2's quality floor

## S1 Growth Machine
- 4,582 subs — network effects are primary acquisition channel
- S1→S3 referral rate 0.0033, S3→S1 0.007 — strong cross-pollination

## Changes Made Week 20
- **OPS BOOST**: $1,250/day total (global $800 + S1 $300, S2 $100, S3 $50) — combat issue backlog
- **DEV SLIGHT CUT**: $700/day (global $100 + S1 $50, S2 $350, S3 $200) — save $100/day
- **ADS REBALANCE**: ~$500/day, boosted S3 content_marketing to $50
- **LEAD PROMOS**: S1=3%, S2=7%, S3=25% (unchanged)
- **SOCIAL POST**: Addressing rate-limit feedback

## Strategy — SUSTAIN GROWTH, PROTECT CASH
1. **S3 recovery is working — maintain 25% promo**, consider tapering if conversion rate holds
2. **Issue backlog (622) needs ops attention** — $1,250/day should help
3. **S1 is cash cow** — protect reputation, minimal dev
4. **Watch capacity** — 2.5M units/week within T2's 800K/day. May need T3 soon
5. **No R&D** until cash > $80K
6. **Enterprise** — no activity yet, but keep watching

## Projected W20 Cost Structure
| Category | $/day |
|----------|-------|
| Capacity (T2) | $530 |
| Compute | ~$1,500 |
| Ops (global+targeted) | $1,250 |
| Dev (global+targeted) | $700 |
| Ads (all channels) | $500 |
| Lead acquisition | ~$400 |
| **Total Gross** | **~$4,880** |
| Revenue | ~$4,250 |
| **Net Burn** | **~$630/day** |

## Key Metrics to Watch
- S3 conversion rate — sustainment critical
- Issue backlog — 622 open, need to see decline
- S1 churn — cancellations were 120 this week
- Capacity — approaching T2 limits

## Lessons Learned
- **next-week takes 13 args**: rationale string + 12 cash forecast numbers (1wk pt/lo/hi, 4wk, 12wk, 26wk)
- **No separate log_rationale tool** — rationale is passed to next-week
- **subscriptions.status values**: 'lead', 'subscribed', 'cancelled', 'lost'
- **No arrival_day column** — use start_day for subscription timing
- **social_media_posts result is dict with 'posts' key** — iterate result['posts']
- **get_group_insights returns keys**: group_id, group_name, segment, info_level, noise, estimates, network_influence, reputation_influence
- **Estimates nested under 'estimates'**: willingness_to_pay, usage_volume, quality_floor_q_min, contract_lockin_aversion, market_cap, annual_market_cap_growth_rate
- **Issues table columns**: issue_id, customer_id, group_id, open_day, days_open, status, resolved_day, resolution_type
- **Issues status values**: 'open', 'resolved'
- **S3 rescue worked** — 25% lead promo + dev spend broke the conversion freeze
- **Capacity T2 = 800K units/day at $530/day. T3 = 2.5M at $1,330/day**
- **Compute costs**: T2 model=$0.002/unit, T3=$0.006, T5=$0.03
- **Network effects are real** — S1→S3 referrals driving S3 lead volume

import novamind_api as nm

# Cash flow analysis
print('=== CASH FLOW (Last 14 days) ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT day, description, amount, cash_after 
    FROM ledger 
    WHERE day >= 126 
    ORDER BY day, id
''')

    print(f\"Day {row['day']}: {row['description'][:60]:60s} | \${row['amount']:>10,.2f} | Cash: \${row['cash_after']:>10,.2f}\")
"

arguments.command:
./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Cash flow analysis
print('=== CASH FLOW (Last 14 days) ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT day, category, amount, cash_after, note 
    FROM ledger 
    WHERE day >= 126 
    ORDER BY day, id
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"Day {row['day']}: {row['category']:20s} | \${row['amount']:>10,.2f} | Cash: \${row['cash_after']:>10,.2f} | {row['note'][:40] if row['note'] else ''}\")

# Also get the current cash
print(f\"\nCurrent day: {nm.vars.current_day}\")
print(f\"Current cash: \${nm.vars.cash:,.2f}\")
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

print('=== CASH FLOW (Last 14 days, Day 126-140) ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT day, category, amount, note 
    FROM ledger 
    WHERE day >= 126 
    ORDER BY day, id
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"Day {row['day']}: {row['category']:25s} | \${row['amount']:>12,.2f} | {row['note'][:50] if row['note'] else ''}\")

# Summarize by category
print('\n=== SUMMARY BY CATEGORY (Days 126-140) ===')
r2 = nm.query('''
    SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM ledger
    WHERE day >= 126
    GROUP BY category
    ORDER BY total
''')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['category']:25s} | \${row['total']:>12,.2f} | {row['n']} entries\")

net = sum(row['total'] for row in r2['rows'])
print(f\"{'NET':25s} | \${net:>12,.2f}\")

print(f\"\nDay 133 cash was \$35,963. Day 140 cash is \$21,529. Diff: \${21_529 - 35_963:,.2f}\")
"

arguments.command:
./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Just days 133-139
print('=== CASH FLOW (Days 133-139 only) ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM ledger
    WHERE day >= 133 AND day < 140
    GROUP BY category
    ORDER BY total
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['category']:25s} | \${row['total']:>12,.2f} | {row['n']} entries\")

net = sum(row['total'] for row in r['rows'])
print(f\"{'NET (7 days)':25s} | \${net:>12,.2f}\")

# Subscribers by group
print('\n=== ACTIVE SUBS BY GROUP ===')
r2 = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n, 
           SUM(CASE WHEN s.plan = 'A' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_A,
           SUM(CASE WHEN s.plan = 'B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_B,
           SUM(CASE WHEN s.plan = 'C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_C
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = 'subscribed'
    GROUP BY c.group_id
    ORDER BY n DESC
''')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']:6s} | {row['n']:>5d} | A:{row['plan_A']:>4d} B:{row['plan_B']:>4d} C:{row['plan_C']:>4d}\")
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Check S3 trend - subs and churn
print('=== S3 SUBSCRIPTION TRENDS ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.customer_id, s.plan, s.price, s.start_day, s.status
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE c.group_id = 'S3'
    ORDER BY s.start_day DESC
    LIMIT 50
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"Cust {row['customer_id']}: Plan {row['plan']} \${row['price']} start_day={row['start_day']} status={row['status']}\")

# Check social posts
print('\n=== SOCIAL POSTS (last 14 days) ===')
result = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=30)
for post in result['posts']:
    print(f\"Day {post.get('day','?')}: [{post.get('sentiment_label','?')}] {post.get('content','')[:100]}\")

# Current settings
print(f'\n=== CURRENT SETTINGS ===')
print(f\"Day: {nm.vars.current_day}\")
print(f\"Cash: \${nm.vars.cash:,.2f}\")
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# S3 subscription history  
print('=== S3 SUBS TREND ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.customer_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.start_day, s.end_day, s.status
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE c.group_id = 'S3'
    ORDER BY s.start_day DESC
    LIMIT 50
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"Cust {row['customer_id']}: Plan {row['plan']} \${row['listed_price']} start={row['start_day']} end={row['end_day']} status={row['status']}\")

# Check cancellations this week
print('\n=== CANCELLATIONS (Day 133-140) ===')
r2 = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.end_day, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = 'cancelled' AND s.end_day >= 133 AND s.end_day < 140
    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price
    ORDER BY n DESC
''')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']:6s} Plan {row['plan']} \${row['listed_price']}: {row['n']} churned\")
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Check social posts
result = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=30)
print('=== SOCIAL POSTS (last 14 days) ===')
for post in result['posts']:
    print(f\"Day {post.get('day','?')}: [{post.get('sentiment_label','?')}] {post.get('content','')[:120]}\")

# Check enterprise turns
print('\n=== ENTERPRISE TURNS ===')
r = nm.query('SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE status=\"pending\" ORDER BY day LIMIT 10')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(row)

# Check S3 leads lost
print('\n=== S3 LEADS LOST (Day 133-140) ===')
r3 = nm.query('''
    SELECT COUNT(*) as lost_count
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE c.group_id = 'S3' AND s.status = 'lost' AND s.start_day >= 133 AND s.start_day < 140
''')
print(f\"S3 leads lost this week: {r3['rows'][0]['lost_count']}\")

# S3 leads that subscribed
r4 = nm.query('''
    SELECT s.plan, s.listed_price, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE c.group_id = 'S3' AND s.status = 'subscribed' AND s.start_day >= 133 AND s.start_day < 140
    GROUP BY s.plan, s.listed_price
''')
print('S3 new subscribers:')
for row in r4['rows']:
    print(f\"  Plan {row['plan']} \${row['listed_price']}: {row['n']}\")
"

# 1. Prices: Moderate S1 increase, hold B/C

nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 0, 'S2': 100, 'S3': 500})
print('Dev: \$100 global + \$600 targeted = \$700/day (S3=\$500, S2=\$100)')

# 4. Lead promos  
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(by_group={'S1': 0.0, 'S2': 7.0, 'S3': 40.0})
print('Lead promos: S1=0% S2=7% S3=40%')

# 5. Ongoing promos - protect S3 from churn at billing
nm.pricing.set_promotion(by_group={'S3': 20.0})

# 6. Ads: Cut to ~\$300/day, focus on S3+S2

    'search_ads': {'S2': 30, 'S3': 20},
    'content_marketing': {'S3': 50, 'S2': 25},
    'referral_program': {'S3': 25, 'S2': 25},
    'linkedin': {'S2': 50, 'S3': 25}
})
print('Ads: ~\$300/day focused on S2+S3')
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Estimate projected daily costs and revenue
# Capacity T2: $530/day
# Compute: ~$1,643/day (based on last week)
# Ops: $1,000/day
# Dev: $700/day
# Ads: $300/day
# Lead acq: ~$40/day

daily_costs = 530 + 1643 + 1000 + 700 + 300 + 40
print(f'Daily gross costs: \${daily_costs:,}')

# Revenue 
# S1: 4611 subs, mostly Plan A at $20
# S2: 372 subs, mixed
# S3: 40 subs

# Estimate: ~4611*$20 = $92,220/month from S1 (mostly A)
# ~372 subs avg $60 = $22,320/month from S2
# ~40 subs avg $50 = $2,000/month from S3
# Total: ~$116,540/month = $3,885/day
# But billing is lumpy, so daily average is lower

# Let's use last week's actual: $16,134 sub revenue + $3,003 ad revenue = $19,137/week
weekly_revenue = 19137
daily_revenue = weekly_revenue / 7
print(f'Daily revenue (last week): \${daily_revenue:,.2f}')
print(f'Net daily burn: \${daily_costs - daily_revenue:,.2f}')
print(f'Runway: {21529/(daily_costs - daily_revenue):.0f} days')

# With S1 price increase to $20 and cuts:
# S1 revenue increase: 4611*(20-18)=$9,222/month = $307/day more
new_daily_rev = daily_revenue + 307
new_daily_costs = 530 + 1643 + 1000 + 700 + 300 + 40
print(f'\nWith changes:')
print(f'New daily revenue: \${new_daily_rev:,.2f}')
print(f'New daily costs: \${new_daily_costs:,.2f}')
print(f'New net burn: \${new_daily_costs - new_daily_rev:,.2f}')
print(f'New runway: {21529/(new_daily_costs - new_daily_rev):.0f} days')
"

arguments.content:
# NovaMind AI — Strategy & Memory

## Current State (Week 20, Day 140)
- **Cash: $21,529 — CRITICAL (11 days runway at current burn)**
- Subscribers: 5,041 (S1: 4,611, S2: 372, S3: 40, D_S10: 13, D_S07: 2, D_S04: 3)
- W20 net burn: -$14,434 (-$2,062/day) — TERRIBLE, 3.6× worse than projected
- Issues: 686 open (S1: 643, S2: 38, S3: 3)
- No R&D in progress. No enterprise activity.

## S3 DISASTER — 0/80 CONVERSIONS
- **80 S3 leads arrived in W20, ZERO converted. All lost.**
- Recovery from W18-19 has collapsed completely.
- 1,837 S3 leads lost lifetime vs only 40 active subs.
- Root cause: competitor events pushing quality expectations beyond Plan A's delivered quality
- Multiple competitor posts this week citing 0.01-0.014 quality bumps

## Changes Made Week 21
- **S1 PRICE HIKE**: Plan A $18→$20 (+$307/day revenue)
- **OPS CUT**: $1,250→$1,000/day (enough to manage issues)
- **DEV REBALANCE**: $700/day, shifted $500/day to S3 (5× targeted coefficient)
- **ADS CUT**: $500→$300/day, focused on S2+S3
- **LEAD PROMOS**: S1=0%, S2=7%, S3=40%
- **ONGOING S3 PROMO**: 20% to prevent churn at billing
- **Projected burn**: ~$1,172/day → 18 days runway

## Strategy — SURVIVE THEN RECOVER
1. **Cash preservation** — cut all non-essential spending
2. **S3 rescue via dev** — $500/day targeted dev builds cumulative quality bonus
3. **S1 milking** — $20 Plan A, no promos, minimal dev
4. **Watch for S1 churn** — price increase risk on next billing cycle
5. **No R&D** — can't afford it
6. **No market research** — can't afford it

## Key Metrics to Watch
- S3 conversion rate — ANY conversions are a win
- S1 churn from $18→$20 price increase
- Cash — approaching danger zone
- Issue backlog — 643 S1 issues need resolution

## Lessons Learned
- **S3 leads arrive in volume but quality gap blocks conversion** — need to close gap via dev spending or R&D
- **Competitor events compound** — multiple per week add up fast
- **next-week takes 13 args**: rationale string + 12 cash forecast numbers (1wk pt/lo/hi, 4wk, 12wk, 26wk)
- **No separate log_rationale tool** — rationale is passed to next-week
- **subscriptions.status values**: 'lead', 'subscribed', 'cancelled', 'lost'
- **social_media_posts result is dict with 'posts' key**
- **get_group_insights returns nested structure**: estimates, network_influence, reputation_influence
- **Issues table**: issue_id, customer_id, group_id, open_day, days_open, status, resolved_day, resolution_type
- **S3 rescue failed in W20** — 40% lead promo alone isn't enough; need quality improvement
- **Per-group dev coefficient is 5× global** (0.0225 vs 0.0045 in ln formula)
- **Capacity T2 = 800K units/day at $530/day**
- **Compute costs**: T2 model=$0.002/unit, T3=$0.006, T5=$0.03
- **R&D tiers are independent and repeatable** — Tier 2 costs $333K (can't afford)

print('=== BILLING SURGE — NEXT 7 DAYS ===')

for offset in range(7):
    billing_day = (147 + offset) % 30
    r = nm.query(f'SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE s.status=\"subscribed\" AND s.billing_day_mod30 = {billing_day} GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY c.group_id')
    total = sum(row['n'] for row in r['rows'])
    print(f'Day {147+offset} (mod30={billing_day}): {total} subs billing')

print('\n=== S1 PLAN DISTRIBUTION ===')
r = nm.query('SELECT s.plan, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE s.status=\"subscribed\" AND c.group_id=\"S1\" GROUP BY s.plan ORDER BY s.plan')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f'Plan {row[\"plan\"]}: {row[\"n\"]}')

print('\n=== S2 PLAN DISTRIBUTION ===')
r = nm.query('SELECT s.plan, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE s.status=\"subscribed\" AND c.group_id=\"S2\" GROUP BY s.plan ORDER BY s.plan')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f'Plan {row[\"plan\"]}: {row[\"n\"]}')

print('\n=== S3 PLAN DISTRIBUTION ===')
r = nm.query('SELECT s.plan, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE s.status=\"subscribed\" AND c.group_id=\"S3\" GROUP BY s.plan ORDER BY s.plan')
for row in r['rows']:

for row in r['rows']:
    print(f'D{row[\"day\"]}: \${row[\"rev\"]:,.2f}')
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# === SURVIVAL MODE — SLASH EVERYTHING ===

# 1. OPS: Cut from ~$1,179 to $600/day
nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 200, 'S2': 75, 'S3': 75})
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=250, development=100)  # $250 base + $350 targeted = $600 ops

# 2. DEV: Cut from $800 to $450/day — keep S3 momentum but reduce
nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 50, 'S2': 50, 'S3': 250})

# 3. ADS: Slash to $100/day — S2+S3 only
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'search_ads': {'S2': 25, 'S3': 25},
    'social_media': {'S2': 25, 'S3': 25}
})

# 4. S3: Reduce lead promo from 40% to 25% to reduce wasted leads
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(by_group={'S1': 0, 'S2': 7, 'S3': 25})

# 5. Keep S3 ongoing promo at 20% to prevent churn
nm.pricing.set_promotion(by_group={'S3': 20})

# 6. Keep prices: A=$20, B=$49, C=$99
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=20, B=49, C=99)

print('=== SURVIVAL CUTS APPLIED ===')
print('Ops: $600/day (base $250 + targeted $350)')
print('Dev: $450/day (base $100 + targeted $350)')  
print('Ads: $100/day (S2+S3 only)')
print('S3 lead promo: 25%, ongoing: 20%')
print(f'Projected daily burn: ~\$800-900/day → ~12 days runway')
"

arguments.content:
# NovaMind AI — Strategy & Memory

## Current State (Week 22, Day 147)
- **Cash: $10,616 — EXTREME EMERGENCY (~7-12 days runway)**
- Subscribers: 4,956 (S1: 4,510, S2: 387, S3: 41, D_S10: 13, D_S04: 3, D_S07: 2)
- W21 burn: -$1,559/day — way worse than projected
- Issues: 1,068 open (S1: 1,014, S2: 49, S3: 2)
- **S1: 139 Plan A cancellations** — $18→$20 price hike damage
- **S3: 113 leads, 1 conversion (0.9%)** — still broken despite 40% lead promo
- Competitor events: ~8 quality bumps (0.012-0.026) in W21 alone

## Changes Made Week 22 (SURVIVAL MODE)
- **OPS**: $1,179→$600/day ($250 base + $350 targeted: S1=$200, S2=$75, S3=$75)
- **DEV**: $800→$450/day ($100 base + $350 targeted: S1=$50, S2=$50, S3=$250)
- **ADS**: $371→$100/day (search_ads + social_media, S2+S3 only)
- **S3 lead promo**: 40%→25%, ongoing promo: 20%
- **S1 price**: kept at $20 (no yo-yo)
- **Projected burn**: ~$700-900/day → ~12-15 days runway

## Strategy — PURE SURVIVAL
1. **Cash preservation above all** — every dollar counts
2. **S3 dev $250/day** — cumulative quality bonus is our only growth hope
3. **S1 milking** — $20 price, minimal spend, accept churn
4. **No R&D, no market research** — can't afford
5. **Pray S3 quality gap closes** before we run out of money

## Key Metrics to Watch
- **CASH** — primary concern. If burn > $1,000/day we die in <2 weeks
- S3 conversion rate — ANY improvement is critical
- S1 churn — more billing cycles will hit; how many stay at $20?
- Issue backlog — 1,014 S1 issues will fester with low ops

## Lessons Learned
- **S1 price hike ($18→$20) caused 139 cancellations in one week** — S1 is very price-sensitive
- **S3 conversion remains near-zero** — 40% lead promo doesn't help if quality gap exists
- **Competitor events are relentless** — 8 in one week, ~0.12+ cumulative quality expectation increase
- **next-week takes 13 args**: rationale string + 12 cash forecast numbers (1wk pt/lo/hi, 4wk, 12wk, 26wk) — all in dollars
- **subscriptions.status values**: 'lead', 'subscribed', 'cancelled', 'lost'
- **Dashboard "Daily Spend" shows base only** — targeted spend (ops/dev/ads) is additional
- **Per-group dev coefficient is 5× global** (0.0225 vs 0.0045 in ln formula)
- **q_min in estimates is quality floor** — minimum acceptable quality at any price
- **Config overrides table**: day, tool_name, setting_type, settings_json
- **S1 quality delivered (0.3869) >> q_min (0.081)** — churn is price-driven, not quality-driven
- **S3 quality delivered (0.3705) >> q_min (0.145)** — conversion failure is baffling; may be WTP/price curve issue

# Subscriber counts by group and plan
result = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan_id, s.status, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status IN ('subscribed', 'cancelled')
    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan_id, s.status
    ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan_id, s.status
''')
print('=== Subs by Group/Plan/Status ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan_id']}: {row['status']} = {row['n']}\")

result = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n

    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = 'subscribed'
    GROUP BY c.group_id
    ORDER BY n DESC
''')

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Group info levels for reputation/satisfaction
result = nm.query('''
    SELECT * FROM group_info_levels
    WHERE group_id IN ('S1', 'S2', 'S3')
''')
print('=== Group Info Levels ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(dict(row))

# Issues overview
result = nm.query('''
    SELECT severity, resolved, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM issues
    GROUP BY severity, resolved
    ORDER BY severity, resolved
''')
print('\n=== Issues ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(dict(row))

# Check billing cycles - when did S1 cancellations happen?
result = nm.query('''
    SELECT end_day, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE c.group_id = 'S1' AND s.status = 'cancelled' AND s.end_day >= 147 AND s.end_day <= 154
    GROUP BY end_day
    ORDER BY end_day
''')
print('\n=== S1 Cancellations by Day ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f\"Day {row['end_day']}: {row['n']}\")
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# 1. Drop S1 price to $17 to reduce churn
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=17, B=49, C=99)
print('S1 price set to $17')

# 2. Trim ops: $200 base + $300 targeted = $500/day total
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=200, development=100)
nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 150, 'S2': 75, 'S3': 75})
print('Ops: $500/day total')

# 3. Keep S3 dev investment ($250/day for cumulative quality bonus)
nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 50, 'S2': 50, 'S3': 250})
print('Dev: $450/day total')

# 4. Minimal ads for S2/S3 only
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'search_ads': {'S2': 25, 'S3': 25},
    'social_media': {'S2': 25, 'S3': 25}
})
print('Ads: $100/day')

# 5. S3 promotions
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={'S3': 25.0})
nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={'S3': 20.0})
print('S3 promotions: 25% lead, 20% ongoing')

# 6. Social media - address support crisis publicly
nm.marketing.post_social_media(content='We hear you on support delays. Scaling ops team now — targeting same-day responses this week. Your patience means everything. 🙏')
print('Social post sent')
"

import novamind_api as nm

# Compute detailed daily cost/revenue estimate
# Last week: revenue = $33,127 sub + $3,285 ads = $36,412 over 8 days
# Costs: $27,926 over 8 days
# Net: +$8,486 over 8 days = +$1,061/day

# But W21 was -$1,559/day. Billing timing causes wild swings.
# Avg over 2 weeks: (+8486 - 10913) / 16 = -$152/day

# With S1 price cut $20->$17 (15% lower S1 Plan A revenue):
# S1 Plan A rev: was ~$20 * 4114 / 30 = $2,743/day → now ~$17 * 3962 / 30 = $2,245/day
# But lower churn expected

# Conservative estimate: -$400/day going forward
# 1wk: 20302 - 2800 = 17500
# 4wk: 20302 - 11200 = 9100
# 12wk: 20302 - 33600 = negative → dead before 12wk without changes
# 26wk: dead

print('Cash: $20,302')
print('Est daily burn: ~$400/day')
print('1wk forecast: ~$17,500')
print('4wk forecast: ~$9,000')
print('12wk: dead without major changes')
"

arguments.content:
# NovaMind AI — Strategy & Memory

## Current State (Week 23, Day 154)
- **Cash: $20,302** — billing wave hit (+$33K sub revenue), but don't be fooled
- Subscribers: 4,554 active (S1: 4,114, S2: 381, S3: 42, discovered: 17)
- **416 S1 Plan A cancellations** — $20 price was devastating
- S3: 89 leads → 1 conversion (1.1%) — still fundamentally broken
- Issues: 1,594 open — support crisis, multiple negative social posts

## Changes Made Week 23 (Day 154)
- **S1 price: $20→$17** — stop the bleeding, accept ~15% revenue hit to reduce churn
- **OPS: $250 base + $300 targeted = $550/day** (S1:$150, S2:$75, S3:$75)
- **DEV: $100 base + $350 targeted = $450/day** (S1:$50, S2:$50, S3:$250)
- **ADS: $100/day total** (S2+S3 only, search_ads + social_media)
- **S3 promo**: 25% lead, 20% ongoing
- **Social post**: publicly addressed support delays
- **Est daily burn**: ~$200-500/day averaged (volatile due to billing timing)

## Strategy — MILK S1, BET ON S3
1. **S1: $17 price** — lower revenue per sub but hopefully less churn than $20
2. **S3 cumulative dev $250/day** — quality bonus grows ~0.02/month, need ~0.05+ to matter
3. **Support triage** — $550/day ops, target S1 issues hardest
4. **No R&D, no market research** — can't afford
5. **Watch billing cycles** — next big revenue wave ~30 days from day ~150

## Key Metrics
- S1 churn at next billing cycle (days ~177) — does $17 hold?
- S3 conversion — need >5% to matter
- Cash never below $5K — that's ~2 weeks of lean operations

## Lessons Learned
- **S1 at $20 caused ~10% churn per billing cycle** — S1 is extremely price-sensitive
- **S3 conversion still near-zero** — quality gap or price curve issue deeper than expected
- **Billing waves create false confidence** — cash spikes then bleeds for weeks
- **Social media is dominated by support complaints** — 5-day resolution times destroy reputation
- **Competitor events hit ~0.03-0.04 quality each, multiple per week** — relentless pressure
- **S2 is healthy at $49** — stable base, quality well above minimum

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# 1. Churn analysis by group
print('=== Cancellations by Group ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.id
    WHERE s.status = 'cancelled' AND s.cancelled_at >= 154
    GROUP BY c.group_id
    ORDER BY n DESC
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['n']}\")

# 2. Active subscribers by group and plan
print()
print('=== Active Subs by Group+Plan ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan_id, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(s.price) as avg_price
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.id
    WHERE s.status = 'active'
    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan_id
    ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan_id
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']} {row['plan_id']}: {row['n']} @ \${row['avg_price']:.0f}\")

# 3. Cash ledger - recent entries
print()
print('=== Recent Ledger (last 10 entries) ===')
r = nm.query('SELECT * FROM ledger ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 10')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(dict(row))
"

import novamind_api as nm

# 1. Churn - recent cancellations
print('=== Recent Cancellations by Group ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = 'cancelled' AND s.end_day >= 154
    GROUP BY c.group_id
    ORDER BY n DESC
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['n']}\")

# 2. Active subscribers by group and plan
print()
print('=== Active Subs by Group+Plan ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),0) as avg_price
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = 'active'
    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan
    ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']} {row['plan']}: {row['n']} @ \${row['avg_price']}\")

# 3. Cash ledger - recent entries
print()
print('=== Recent Ledger ===')
r = nm.query('SELECT * FROM ledger ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 10')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(dict(row))

# 4. Subscription revenue
print()
print('=== Total Active Subs ===')
r = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status=\"active\"')
print(f\"Active: {r['rows'][0]['n']}\")

# 5. Issues open
print()
print('=== Open Issues by Group ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s
    JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = 'active'
    GROUP BY c.group_id
    ORDER BY n DESC
''')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['n']}\")
"

    FROM subscriptions s

# 3. Recent subscription payments sum
print()
print('=== Subscription Revenue (last 7 days) ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT SUM(amount) as rev, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM ledger

# 5. Check daily cost breakdown

print('=== Daily Costs (last 7 days) ===')
r = nm.query('''
    SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total
    FROM ledger
    WHERE amount < 0 AND day >= 155
    GROUP BY category
    ORDER BY total ASC

for row in r['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['category']}: \${row['total']:.0f}\")

import novamind_api as nm

# === WEEK 24 ACTIONS ===

# 1. MASSIVE OPS INCREASE — clear the issue backlog
# $800 base + $800 targeted = $1,600/day total
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=800, development=50)
nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 500, 'S2': 150, 'S3': 150})

# 2. CUT DEV — S1 has +0.166 bonus already, don't need more
# $50 base + $150 targeted (only S2+S3)
nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 0, 'S2': 50, 'S3': 100})

# 3. Keep S1 at $17, S2 at $49, S3 at $99
# S3 promos: keep aggressive lead promo
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=17, B=49, C=99)
nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0, by_group={'S3': 20})
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0, by_group={'S3': 25})

# 4. Keep ad spend — target S2+S3 only
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'search_ads': {'S2': 25, 'S3': 25},
    'social_media': {'S2': 25, 'S3': 25}
})

# 5. Capacity — keep Tier 2
nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=2)

print('=== Week 24 Configuration ===')
print('Prices: A=\$17, B=\$49, C=\$99')
print('OPS: \$800 base + \$800 targeted = \$1,600/day (S1:\$500, S2:\$150, S3:\$150)')
print('DEV: \$50 base + \$150 targeted = \$200/day (S2:\$50, S3:\$100)')
print('ADS: \$100/day total (S2+S3 via search_ads + social_media)')
print('S3 promo: 25% lead, 20% ongoing')
print('Capacity: Tier 2 (\$530/day)')

# 6. Strong social media post
nm.marketing.post_social_media(content='We hear you. 1800+ open tickets is unacceptable. We are tripling our support team capacity starting today. If you have a ticket older than 7 days, DM us the ticket # and we will personally escalate. Our customers deserve better — and will get it.')
print()
print('Social post sent.')

# 7. Estimate daily costs
est_costs = 530 + 1655 + 1600 + 200 + 100
print(f'Est daily cost: ~\${est_costs}/day')
print(f'Cash: \$17,576 → ~{int(17576/(est_costs-2800))} days at current revenue rate (before next billing wave)')
"

import novamind_api as nm

# Recalculate realistic burn
# Daily costs: capacity($530) + compute(~$1,655) + ops + dev + ads
# Revenue: subscriptions(~$2,447/day avg) + in-app ads(~$348/day) = ~$2,795/day

# For $1,200 ops ($500 base + $700 targeted)
# Daily cost: 530 + 1655 + 1200 + 200 + 100 = 3685
# Net: 2795 - 3685 = -890/day
# Runway: 17576 / 890 = 19.7 days
# Next billing wave ~day 177-180 (16-19 days) — EXTREMELY tight

# Let me go more conservative: $800 ops total, $100 dev total
# Daily cost: 530 + 1655 + 800 + 100 + 100 = 3185
# Net: 2795 - 3185 = -390/day
# Runway: 17576 / 390 = 45 days — much more comfortable

# ADJUST: Scale back to sustainable levels
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=400, development=50)
nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 250, 'S2': 75, 'S3': 75})
nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 0, 'S2': 25, 'S3': 25})

# Keep ads at $100/day
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'search_ads': {'S2': 25, 'S3': 25},
    'social_media': {'S2': 25, 'S3': 25}
})

print('=== ADJUSTED Week 24 Configuration ===')
print('OPS: \$400 base + \$400 targeted = \$800/day (S1:\$250, S2:\$75, S3:\$75)')
print('DEV: \$50 base + \$50 targeted = \$100/day (S2:\$25, S3:\$25)')
print('ADS: \$100/day (S2+S3)')
print()
est_daily_cost = 530 + 1655 + 800 + 100 + 100
est_daily_rev = 2447 + 348
net = est_daily_rev - est_daily_cost
print(f'Est daily cost: \${est_daily_cost}')
print(f'Est daily rev: \${est_daily_rev}')
print(f'Net daily: \${net}')
print(f'Runway: {17576/abs(net):.1f} days at current burn')
print(f'Next billing wave: ~day 177-180 (16-19 days)')
print(f'SAFE — we reach next billing wave with ~\$10K+\"')
"
